summary: Descriptive overview of estatesinland in substantive law: fee simple, life estate, defeasible fees, future interests.
key_points: fee simple is a fundamental concept in property law.
Legal standards for estatesinland are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine estatesinland in litigation.
The doctrine of estatesinland has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding estatesinland requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
sources: Supreme Court opinions (Kelo v. New London)
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
